title: Arrays, Slices, Backing Storage, Append, Copy, and Capacity · Runtime Contract: Slicing Full Expressions
summary: A batch processor must window records, grow buffers, preserve caller data, and avoid retention or aliasing defects. Planned artifact: Add and verify the go slicing full expressions increment in an alias-safe batch and buffer toolkit. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use two-index and full slice expressions while proving bounds, capacity, and append authority.
Reason about slice length, capacity, backing storage, nil slices, empty non-nil slices, and observable API differences.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Slicing Full Expressions · Theory Slicing Full Expressions
steps.instruction:
theory case — A batch slice exposes 7 of 11 records from shared storage. Append one result, mutate one element, clone one boundary, and predict length, capacity, aliasing, and retained storage after every operation.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Slicing Full Expressions contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Slicing Full Expressions · Theory Slicing Full Expressions.

For Predict Slicing Full Expressions · Theory Slicing Full Expressions, keep Slicing Full Expressions connected to executable Go behavior evidence.
